content.problemStatement:
For a string SSS let the unique set of characters that occur in it one or more times be CCC. Consider a permutation of the elements of CCC as (c1,c2,c3...)(c_1, c_2, c_3 ... )(c1​,c2​,c3​...). Let f(c)f(c)f(c) be the number of times ccc occurs in SSS.

If any such permutation of the elements of CCC satisfies f(ci)=f(ci−1)+f(ci−2)f(c_i) = f(c_{i-1}) + f(c_{i-2})f(ci​)=f(ci−1​)+f(ci−2​) for all i≥3i \ge 3i≥3, the string is said to be a **dynamic string**.

Mr Bancroft is given the task to check if the string is dynamic, but he is busy playing with sandpaper. Would you help him in such a state?

Note that if the number of distinct characters in the string is less than 3, i.e. if ∣C∣<3|C| < 3∣C∣<3, then the string is always dynamic.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, a string SSS.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line "**Dynamic**" if the given string is dynamic, otherwise print "**Not**". (Note that the judge is case sensitive)

###Constraints


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤∣S∣≤1051 \leq |S| \leq 10^51≤∣S∣≤105
SSS contains only lower case alphabets: aaa, bbb, ..., zzz
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of a single line containing a string S.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output 'Dynamic' if the given string is dynamic, otherwise print 'Not'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the string 'aaaabccc' has a unique character set of {a, b, c}. The frequency of each character is {a: 4, b: 1, c: 3}. The permutation (b, c, a) satisfies the Fibonacci sequence condition, so the string is dynamic. For the second test case, the string 'aabbcc' has a unique character set of {a, b, c}. However, no permutation of the characters satisfies the Fibonacci sequence condition, so the string is not dynamic. For the third test case, the string 'ppppmmnnoooopp' has a unique character set of {m, n, o, p}. The permutation (m, n, o, p) satisfies the Fibonacci sequence condition, so the string is dynamic.

solution.approach: To determine if a string is dynamic, we need to check all permutations of its unique characters. For each permutation, we calculate the frequency of each character and check if it satisfies the Fibonacci sequence condition. If any permutation satisfies the condition, the string is dynamic.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * |S| * |C|!) — where T is the number of test cases, |S| is the length of the string, and |C| is the number of unique characters in the string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S| + |C|) — for storing the string and the frequency of each character.
